what_it_does: Panel re-jury of jury-flagged registry entries in reconciled.json. For each class 'jury' entry, it rebuilds the real element unit (cluster_members into candidates order) and runs the registration_confirm panel (three lenses: grounding, voice, element-fit; quorum 2/3, temperature-0). Verdict True reclassifies the entry to 'confirmed'; verdict False reclassifies to 'panel' (stays flagged with named seat dissents). Entry-idempotent (entries already carrying 'panel' are skipped), time-budgeted, checkpointed, fail-loud per entry. Proposes classes only; reconciled.json updated in place.
observations: Uses PanelRegistry().discover().get('registration_confirm') and C.run_panel with a resolve_role callback into the suite's role registry.
element_of resolves the unit via the source mockup's artifact, cluster_members[0], and candidates filtered by mockup_file.
The rationale: jury-class means floor already passed; the old no-quorum was draw-variance, and the temperature-0 panel supersedes the old same-role draw-jury.
Records {'error':..., 'verdict': None} into the panel field when the element is unrebuildable or the panel raises — never silent.
Default time budget 600s; checkpoints reconciled.json and prints progress every 10 judged.
Recounts all classes at the end and reports judged/failed/remaining/counts.
standouts: The conceptual claim that a temperature-0 multi-lens panel resolves the prior jury's variance (no-quorum was noise, not judgment).
Verdict-False produces the 'panel' class that rg10_redescribe.py then consumes — an explicit hand-off between passes.
Strict context law: the fit-lens is given the real element, because a fit-lens without the element would rightly dissent.
conventions: Idempotent + checkpointed + time-budgeted, matching the redescribe sibling.
Same chdir/sys.path bootstrap as the other rg10 passes (no design/_system path here, since no register_element re-fire).
concerns: element_of duplicates almost verbatim the same helper in rg10_redescribe.py and rg10_reask.py — three near-identical copies of the artifact/cluster resolution logic.
Broad `except Exception` around run_panel collapses error detail into a 200-char string.
Reclassification depends entirely on the panel's verdict field shape (verdict/grounded_seats/quorum/seats) — tight coupling to run_panel's return contract.
notes: Re-jury: runs the temperature-0 registration_confirm panel on jury-flagged entries, promoting agreeing ones to confirmed and demoting disagreeing ones to the 'panel' class for re-describe.
questions: What are the three registration_confirm lenses' exact pass criteria and how is quorum 2/3 computed in run_panel?
Why does this pass not re-fire register_element while redescribe does — is the jury class assumed content-correct already?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the docstring's panel re-jury, confirmed/panel reclassification, idempotency, and propose-only behavior are all implemented.
apparent_themes: Context law (the lens must see the real element).
Explicit class hand-off between pipeline passes.
intention_signals: Tries to rescue entries the original jury couldn't agree on by re-judging them with a deterministic temperature-0 panel that sees the real element, promoting the agreeable ones and routing the rest to the re-describe stage, so Tim's gate receives the smallest refined set.
novelty: Reframing prior no-quorum results as variance and re-adjudicating with a temperature-0 element-grounded panel — distinguishing judgment from sampling noise — is a distinctive re-jury rationale.
contribution: Contributes the panel re-jury stage — the temperature-0, element-grounded re-adjudication of jury-flagged entries that promotes confirms and emits the 'panel' class consumed downstream.
summary_for_embedding: A time-budgeted Python pass that re-juries jury-flagged registry entries with a temperature-0 three-lens registration_confirm panel, promoting agreeing entries to confirmed and demoting the rest to the panel class.
intention_for_embedding: To re-adjudicate entries the original jury couldn't agree on using a deterministic element-grounded panel, separating real judgment from sampling variance.
